frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: compare similarity patterns of cortical activity to similarity patterns from pose models

frameworks.name: 3D pose reconstruction
frameworks.one_line: applied to natural-scene images to build view-independent body-pose model

core_question: How does the human brain represent body pose, and does the representation reflect 3D view-independent structure?
key_assumptions: spatial relationships between body parts constitute pose information
model-derived similarity structure can be compared to fMRI similarity structure
natural complex scenes provide a valid stimulus space for pose perception
manipulations: depth availability of pose model (2D vs 3D)
viewpoint dependency of pose model (view-dependent vs view-independent)
pose variation across >4,000 complex natural scenes
key_findings: high-resolution fMRI responses correlate significantly with pose-model similarity patterns
the 3D view-independent pose model fit cortical activity best
diverse cortical representations span 2D and 3D pose
interpretations: cortical body-pose representations extend beyond body-part detection to relational, view-independent 3D structure
